dataset: AdminPilot Top 50 US Vehicle Maintenance Schedules
schema_notes: A null mileage or month value means the manufacturer does not specify that dimension.
Condition-based oil-life and maintenance-minder systems remain condition-based; fixed mileage is not invented.
Severe-use intervals are labeled conditional and must not be presented as normal-use requirements.
Combined model families and materially different powertrains are flagged for Phase 3 splitting.

reason: Official PDF was portal-restricted in this extraction environment.
Confirm cabin-filter and spark-plug intervals against the exact 2021 DT manual.
Portal restriction prevented direct official PDF extraction; aggregator copy may be used only as a flagged fallback.

Off-road and water-crossing service cannot be represented by mileage alone.
Confirm performance-engine spark-plug interval and transmission schedule.

phase3_priorities: Recheck low-confidence Mopar records against exact VIN/year manuals.
Split hybrid, EV, diesel, turbo, transmission, and drivetrain variants where flagged.
Confirm long-life coolant and spark-plug intervals that sit at manufacturer table boundaries.
Preserve condition-based Honda, Ford, GM, Mercedes, and oil-monitor logic.
Do not convert severe-use transmission intervals into normal maintenance.

interval.logic: normal use example range is 7,500-10,000 miles; severe use shortens to 5,000-7,500 miles; extreme use 3,000-5,000 miles

maintenance.notes: Ford uses the Intelligent Oil-Life Monitor; the manual says actual intervals depend on severity of use. Do not exceed one year or 10,000 miles between service intervals.
